idea.summary: A curated PWA app store where non-technical people discover simple, affordable tools for specific everyday needs — tools too niche for big companies to build, now possible because of AI-assisted development.
idea.founder_context: Founder has firsthand experience in startup Reddit communities, sees massive supply of vibe coders desperate for distribution. Noticed Apple tightening policies against vibecoded apps, creating a displacement opportunity. Wants to build a PWA-first download system that sidesteps native app store gatekeepers.
initial_assumptions: Apple's tightening of vibecoded app policies creates real displacement — rejected apps need a home
Vibe coders are desperate for distribution and will list on a new platform with low friction
Non-technical users will trust and install PWAs from a curated third-party store
There's a meaningful long tail of niche use cases that big SaaS won't serve
Curation/quality control is achievable without Apple-level review costs
PWAs are good enough for most of these niche use cases (no need for native)
Discovery (not just listing) is the core value — users come with a need and find a tool
open_questions: How familiar are non-technical users with PWAs? Is the install UX a barrier?
What's the actual volume of vibecoded apps being rejected or not submitted to Apple/Google?
How would curation work at scale — community ratings, editorial, automated quality checks?
What's the monetization model — listing fees, revenue share, freemium for devs, ads?
Are there existing competitors doing this or adjacent plays (PWA stores, indie app directories)?
What categories of niche tools have the strongest demand signal?
Can you build trust with non-technical users without the Apple/Google brand backing the store?
idea.value_proposition: Non-technical people get a single place to discover and install simple, cheap, niche tools that don't exist on traditional app stores — powered by the explosion of AI-built software that Apple and Google won't distribute.
possible_icp: Non-technical solopreneurs and freelancers who need simple niche tools but find mainstream SaaS overkill/expensive
Small business owners looking for cheap single-purpose utilities
Non-technical everyday consumers who want a specific app for a specific micro-need (e.g. pet feeding schedule, niche calculator, local event tracker)
Vibe coders / AI-assisted developers who need distribution for their apps (supply side)
